mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail and food services sales directly captures nominal consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of months when RSAFS is rising, or Rank IC between RSAFS changes and forward returns is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5% reflects restrictive monetary policy that raises the equity discount rate and increases the probability of a hard landing, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in over 50% of weeks when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, or the mean forward return during triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts generate a reinforcing demand impulse where consumer spending strength meets residential investment expansion, amplifying positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint z-score interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST exhibits a Rank IC below 0.04 over any 3-year window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of joint-up months.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 4% during late-cycle expansion signals deteriorating labor market momentum that precedes consumption slowdowns and earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 records positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4%, or the conditional Sharpe ratio during this regime is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production indicates improving manufacturing momentum and capacity utilization, which lifts cyclical earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of weeks when INDPRO is accelerating, or the Pearson correlation between INDPRO changes and 5-day forward returns is negative over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrently elevated consumer price inflation and federal funds rate above 5% create a stagflationary squeeze that elevates real discount rates and compresses profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS produces a Rank IC above 0.02 in favor of positive returns, or negative returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth provides a fundamental anchor for aggregate corporate cash flows; positive growth surprises translate into upward earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns follow positive GDPC1 surprises in fewer than 55% of quarters, or the regression beta of GDP surprises on equity returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll gain exceeding 200 thousand during post-recession recovery signals rapid labor market repair and accelerating household income, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers negative 5-day returns in over 50% of months when PAYEMS rises more than 200k from the prior month, or the mean weekly return during triggered months is not positive.
